data.title: Revvity expects to report Q4 revenue of $772 million
data.description:
• Revvity (RVTY) on Tuesday said that it expects to post fourth quarter revenue of about $772 million (vs consensus of $759.24M) and full year revenue of ~$2.86 billion (vs consensus of 2.84B).
  • It has projected reported and organic Y/Y revenue growth of  around  6% and 4%, respectively, for the fourth quarter. For the full year 2025, reported and organic revenue growth is expected to be around 4% and 3%, respectively, 
  • The company also now expects its full year adjusted earnings per share to exceed the upper-end of the $4.90-$5.00 guidance (vs consensus of $4.91) provided on October 27, 2025.
  • RVTY is +8.70% to $112.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Check Point, Varonis in focus as Citi adds negative Catalyst Watch; Rapid7 downgraded
data.description:
Check Point Software (CHKP) and Varonis (VRNS) were in the spotlight on Tuesday as Citi added a negative Catalyst Watch to both cybersecurity companies. Additionally, Rapid7 (RPD) was downgraded by the firm.

For Check Point, analyst Fatima Boolani said the analyst consensus is “under-modeling” the company's expenses in 2026, which could hurt operating margins, estimated to be around 40%. “First-ever $2B convert issuance indicates more and perhaps big M&A likely to be further dilutive, further reinforcing our reservations,” Boolani wrote in a note to clients. In addition, Check Point and Fortinet (FTNT) could both experience some negatives in the short-term. However, sentiment could flip in the second-half of the year, as Fortinet offers the potential for re-acceleration against a “high short interest,” though rising memory prices may keep a lid on gross margins, Boolani posited.

For Varonis, Boolani said the company checks “several boxes” as a potential acquisition candidate, but growing competition in the data security space risks buyers pausing their intentions. “Coupled with realized business churn, CY26E [net new annual recurring revenue] is likely uneven, where guide is likely hedged, staying flattish,” Boolani said.

Boolani downgraded Rapid7 to Neutral from Buy, as she believes the current risk-reward profile “looks balanced for an asset expected to deliver [low-single-digit] topline growth, with muted overall P&L upside and challenging secular/competitive/GTM standing, mitigated partially by activist involvement.” Boolani also moved her price target on Rapid7 to $15 from $25.

data.title: Draganfly drones to assist with Sweden's search and rescue operations
data.description:
Draganfly (DRPO) shares are getting a lift at Tuesday’s open as the company’s drones have been  selected to assist Europe’s leading search-and-rescue ((SAR)) organizations with aerial support.  

The drones will be outfitted with Smith Myers, ARTEMIS Mobile Phone Detection & Location Systems ((MPDLS)) and deployed with Search and Rescue Sweden to assist in missing-person recoveries, wilderness rescues, police support missions, and other critical public-safety tasks.

Equipped with the ARTEMIS MPDLS, the drones will have a maximum range of 35km while supporting up to 48 cellular bands. The Smith Myers payloads integrated with Draganfly (DRPO) drones provides flexibility, lighter, more mobile APEX aircraft with a smaller payload enabling rapid deployment and reconnaissance.

Draganfly (DPRO) shares extended their rally for a fourth straight session, rising another 3%, as momentum builds on Washington’s proposed $1.5T defense budget for FY27 and policy shifts that could favor reinvestment over capital returns.

data.title: Memory supply crunch affects PC, smartphone, auto markets in 2026: KeyBanc
data.description:
The ongoing memory supply crunch due to insatiable data center demand will likely create risks across the PC, smartphone and automotive markets in 2026, according to KeyBanc.

"With data center bit growth expected at +50% in 2026, hyperscalers are securing significant DRAM and NAND supply, driving contract pricing up +25% in 1Q26 and +10-12% in 2Q26 for DRAM, and +20% and +10-15%, respectively, for NAND," said KeyBanc analysts John Vinh and Ryan Rosumny in an investor report. "Outside of the data center, fulfillment rates vs. forecasted demand are tracking at just 20-45%, leaving OEMs in PCs and smartphones struggling to secure capacity."

This is expected to cause PC unit shipments to decline from 5% to 10% throughout the year and smartphone units to decline by 3% to 5%. This could also prompt Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (SSNLF) to increase prices by $100 to $150 per device.

"Competition for LPDDR5X intensifies as NVDA's Vera CPU consumes 1.5TB per system (~20B Gb), equivalent to memory for 100M-150M smartphones (just under 10% of the smartphone market)," Vinh and Rosumny added.

These developments, however, prove positive for a range of semiconductor companies, such as Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), Micron (MU), Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR), Marvell (MRVL), Cirrus Logic (CRUS), Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) and Analog Devices (ADI). However, they could be negatives for Qualcomm (QCOM) and Arm (ARM).

KeyBanc increased Micron's price target to $450 from $325, Lattice to $95 from $85, and Analog Devices to $375 from $330.

data.title: Extreme Networks exploring purchase of Ruckus from CommScope - report
data.description:
Extreme Networks (EXTR) is considering an acquisition of Ruckus Networks from CommScope (COMM).

Extreme (EXTR) has been looking at a cash-and-stock deal that could value the provider of Wi-Fi and networking systems at more than $1 billion, according to a Bloomberg report  on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

A final decision on a deal for Ruckus hasn’t been made and Extreme could still decide against moving forward, according to the report. Extreme and CommScope didn’t respond to Bloomberg requests for comment.

Extreme Networks rose 1.2%, while CommScope gained 1%.

data.title: Revvity trades higher on earnings guidance above consensus
data.description:
Revvity (RVTY) stock was surging after the health science solutions provider said it expects full-year adjusted EPS to exceed the upper end of the $4.90-$5.00 guidance previously provided.

The 2025 consensus EPS estimate stands at $4.91.

Shares were +8.97% to $113.21 during Tuesday morning trading.

For the fourth quarter, the Waltham-based company expects revenue of ~$772M, compared to the consensus of 759.24M.

For the full year, the company estimates revenue of ~$2.86B, against the consensus of $2.84B.

data.title: Arm's rating cut at BofA ahead of earnings results
data.description:
BofA downgraded the rating of British chip architecture designer Arm (ARM) to Neutral from Buy and lowered the price target on the stock to $120 from $145 ahead of the company's third quarter fiscal year 2026 results on Feb. 4.

Shares of Arm dipped about 1% on Tuesday.

The analysts said the downgraded Arm on near-term smartphone unit headwinds (memory cost), increasing reliance on SoftBank (SFTBY) (SFTBF) in licensing, and still small data center and AI exposure (only about 10% of royalties).

Arm is majority-owned by SoftBank.

The analysts flag revenue slowdown (both royalties/licensing) and increasing SoftBank reliance into 20226. Particularly, global smartphone units could decline low single digits year-over-year (versus up low single digits in 2025) on increased memory costs and supply constraints, a headwind to Arm Client (>50% of royalty sales).

"Meanwhile, CSS adoption (or content expansion) is still limited and in early stages. For Licensing, we flag FY26 revenue could actually decline -5% YoY (with the most recent FQ2 sales missing by $70mn) if we exclude SoftBank which now represents 25-30% of total Licensing and could raise circular financing concerns. Longer-term though, we continue to like ARM’s potential in data center," said the analysts.

Separately, BofA provided a preview of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC) ahead of the companies respective earnings results. Intel reports on Jan, 22, while AMD reports on Feb. 3.

The analysts said they expect AI/servers to be the stronghold for compute, with AMD likely to beat/raise on quickly growing data center exposure and strong product execution (Mi355X ramping).

The analysts also expect Intel to benefit from healthy server shipments (up high-teens year-over-year in 2025) in the fourth quarter, but see gross margin, or GM, headwinds in the first quarter from higher memory prices and ongoing portfolio transition to less profitable Intel 3/4 nodes from Intel 7/10.

data.title: Performance Shipping announces placement of $50M tap issue
data.description:
• Performance Shipping (PSHG) on Tuesday said it has placed a $50 million tap issue in its outstanding senior secured bond due July 17, 2029, paying a fixed coupon of 9.875% per annum, payable semi-annually in arrears.
  • Following the tap issue, the total outstanding amount under the Bonds is $150 million. The tap issue was priced at 103.00% of par value and was initiated by a reverse inquiry.
  • The tap issue is expected to close on January 26, 2026.
  • Net proceeds from the tap issue will be used for general corporate purposes according to the terms of the Bonds.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock market largely flat as retail inflation comes in slightly cooler
data.description:
Wall Street was unchanged on Tuesday as core CPI inflation came in slightly cooler than expected. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.1%, the Dow (DJI) -0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) were all flat. 

Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) down 1 basis point to 3.52%. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was largely flat at 4.17%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was also unchanged at 4.83%. 

The December Consumer Price Index came in at +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.7% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.7% prior.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy) came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

data.title: Signing Day Sports prices $5.6 million public offering of stocks and warrants
data.description:
• Signing Day Sports (SGN) said on Tuesday it priced a public offering of 9.48 million shares of common stock, together with warrants to purchase 14.23 million shares, at $0.5905 per share and accompanying warrant.
  • The warrants are initially exercisable at $0.7086 per share and expire in five years or upon full exercise.
  • Gross proceeds from the offering are expected to total $5.6 million, before underwriting fees and expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 14, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • SGN -59.88% premarket to $0.2369.
  • Source: Press Release
